mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, independent of any threshold or regime condition.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 across any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, this signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical instances where the trigger fires, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive, or Rank IC in triggered subsample is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signals broad-based consumer and residential investment strength that is not captured by either series alone, driving above-trend GDP growth expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND HOUST 3-month change > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger subsample shows no statistically significant difference (p > 0.10) in 60-day forward returns versus the single-series conditioned subsamples, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals capacity constraints and impending margin compression for industrial firms, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this relationship is muted or reversed during early-cycle recoveries when INDPRO deceleration reflects normalization rather than distress.
failure_condition: The negative-return relationship between INDPRO deceleration and 60-day forward SPX returns fails to hold (coefficient sign flips or loses significance at p<0.10) when restricted to periods with UNRATE below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS growth reflects strengthening labor income and consumption capacity, feeding through to near-term equity risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds 4%, markets price in an increased probability of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month until inflation moderates.
failure_condition: In periods where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 4%, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC in this subsample is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in GDPC1 quarterly growth combined with declining RSAFS signals a synchronized weakening of aggregate demand across both the macro and micro consumption channels, forecasting a negative S&P 500 return over the following quarter that neither series predicts reliably in isolation.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth < 1% AND RSAFS 3-month change < 0%
failure_condition: The joint-trigger subsample fails to produce 60-day forward returns significantly more negative (p > 0.10) than either single-series-triggered subsample, indicating absence of a true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a low and stable unemployment regime (UNRATE below 5.5%), further declines in UNRATE reinforce a tight labor market narrative that supports near-term consumer spending confidence, producing modest positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following trading week; in high-unemployment regimes, further declines instead signal cyclical recovery risk repricing with weaker or reversed effect.
regime_hint: low unemployment / tight labor market regime
failure_condition: The positive relationship between UNRATE declines and 5-day forward SPX returns fails to hold or reverses sign when UNRATE is below 5.5%, tested via rolling 2-year regression with p > 0.10.
